Low MCH can be seen in microcytic anemia associated with Crohn's disease.
Low MCH/MCHC can be seen in microcytic anemia associated with Crohn's disease.
Microcytic anemia (low MCV) can be seen in Crohn's disease due to iron deficiency from blood loss or malabsorption.
Thrombocytopenia can occur in Crohn's disease due to splenic sequestration, bone marrow suppression, or bleeding.

Elevated RDW can indicate anemia of chronic inflammation, a common finding in Crohn's disease.
Elevated WBC count can indicate inflammation or infection, which can be associated with Crohn's disease.
